title: Blue Cedar Trial
solution: Sonia Hale discovered that Blue Cedar Neurology Center was relabeling expired infusion vials and suppressing adverse-event data tied to a sponsor-funded migraine drug trial. Medical director Dr. Adrian Quill intercepted her after she copied internal records, sedated her in Records Annex B, used a master override to conceal the badge trail, and hid her alive in a decommissioned sleep-study room in the lower diagnostic wing. The case is solved by badge access logs, cold-chain inventory discrepancies, parking garage video, sedative residue, and Quill's staged conference alibi.

suspects.name: Eli Rowan
suspects.alibi: On ambulance standby at Station 4 from 5:00 PM to 9:10 PM
suspects.motive: Recent breakup and anger over money Sonia still owed him
what_they_know: Sonia told him she was scared of what she found in the clinic freezer logs
Sonia said a doctor at Blue Cedar was changing records after patient reactions
what_they_lie_about: He says Sonia never mentioned being afraid of anyone at work
He downplays how hostile their last argument became

suspects.name: Marisol Kent
suspects.alibi: Covering infusion chairs and late charting until after 8:15 PM
suspects.motive: Sonia had threatened to report unsigned infusion notes and protocol shortcuts on Marisol's shift
what_they_know: Sonia was reconciling adverse-event binders the day she vanished
Dr. Quill told staff not to discuss patient seizure events with sponsor auditors
what_they_lie_about: Marisol says Sonia was only doing routine paperwork and nothing unusual
She omits that she directed Sonia to finish work alone in Records Annex B

suspects.name: Felix Dorn
suspects.alibi: Completing evening medication counts in the basement pharmacy from 5:40 PM to 7:50 PM
suspects.motive: Sonia had questioned why his initials appeared on relabeled trial vials and missing cold-chain logs
what_they_know: He saw Sonia photographing freezer shelves and lot stickers that afternoon
He saw Dr. Quill request replacement labels for infusion stock two days earlier
what_they_lie_about: He says there were no inventory discrepancies in the trial freezer
He denies noticing any relabeled or expired vials

suspects.name: Dr. Adrian Quill
suspects.alibi: At a regional neurology conference dinner from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM
suspects.motive: Sonia uncovered trial fraud involving relabeled expired infusions and suppressed adverse-event reports, and Quill silenced her to avoid criminal exposure, loss of license, and sponsor collapse
what_they_know: He sedated Sonia in Records Annex B after she copied the adverse-event spreadsheet
He moved Sonia by transport chair into the decommissioned lower diagnostic wing
He left his phone and conference badge near colleagues to support a false alibi
what_they_lie_about: He says he left the clinic before 5:30 PM and never returned that evening
He says Sonia had no access to sensitive trial records
He denies any expired or relabeled infusion stock existed at Blue Cedar

timeline.event: Sonia badges into Blue Cedar Neurology Center and opens the trial records office
Sonia tells Eli by text that the infusion lot numbers and patient reaction counts do not match
Marisol instructs Sonia to finish the adverse-event reconciliation and leave the binders in Records Annex B
Sonia copies the active adverse-event spreadsheet to a thumb drive
Sonia's badge is used to enter Records Annex B
Unsent email draft appears on Sonia's laptop: 'If something happens check Quill and freezer log 4C'
Parking garage camera captures Dr. Quill's vehicle returning to the clinic
A security officer sees Quill pushing a covered transport chair toward the lower diagnostic wing
Quill's phone remains active at the conference hotel while his clinic master credential is used on the service elevator
timeline.location: Hilton Medical Center Hotel and Blue Cedar Neurology Center
timeline.source: Cell record and access control review

timeline.event: Sonia is reported missing after failing to return home and missing scheduled data submission
Investigators find benzodiazepine residue, a torn badge lanyard, and scuff marks in Records Annex B
Warrant team locates Sonia alive inside a locked decommissioned sleep-study room in the lower diagnostic wing

evidence.description: Sonia's final recorded badge entry is into Records Annex B at 6:03 PM
evidence.points_to: The disappearance occurred inside the clinic after normal work hours

evidence.description: Trial freezer 4C contains expired infusion vials covered with fresh lot stickers and handwritten relabel dates
evidence.points_to: Internal medical fraud tied to trial inventory

evidence.description: Parking garage camera shows Dr. Quill returning to Blue Cedar after he claimed he had already left for the conference
Sonia exported the adverse-event spreadsheet minutes before she vanished
evidence.points_to: She discovered material serious enough to preserve outside the system

evidence.description: Unsent email draft reads: 'If something happens check Quill and freezer log 4C'
evidence.points_to: Sonia feared a specific person and linked him to the inventory issue

evidence.description: Security officer reports seeing Quill move a covered transport chair toward the lower diagnostic wing shortly after Sonia's last badge use
evidence.points_to: Victim was moved deeper into the clinic by Quill

evidence.description: Quill's phone remained active at the conference hotel while his clinic master credential opened a service elevator at Blue Cedar
Midazolam residue, adhesive transfer, and a torn badge lanyard are recovered from Records Annex B
evidence.points_to: Sonia was chemically subdued and removed against her will

evidence.description: Investigators recover Sonia alive from a locked decommissioned sleep-study room along with her missing thumb drive
evidence.points_to: Victim was concealed inside Blue Cedar after being abducted on-site

evidence.description: Hidden binders from Quill's office contain suppressed seizure reports, overwritten timestamps, and sponsor copies that do not match the internal record

documents.content: Reporting party: Marisol Kent. Sonia Hale failed to return home and missed mandatory trial upload. Vehicle remains at clinic. Purse and laptop charger located in records office. No indication of voluntary departure.
Eli Rowan states Sonia only complained about stress and never said she was in danger. Text review later shows Sonia directly referenced false lot numbers and being afraid of someone at work.
Marisol to Sonia at 2:26 PM: Finish the adverse-event binders tonight. Leave everything in Annex B and lock up when done.
Night security officer first reports nothing unusual, then admits he saw Dr. Quill moving a covered transport chair into the lower diagnostic corridor and assumed it was equipment.
Felix Dorn's internal note flags two infusion lots with mismatched expiration records and replacement labels, but the memo was never submitted to compliance.
Conference staff recall Quill's phone and badge remained at the dinner table while he was absent for a substantial period between 6:30 PM and 7:15 PM.

Search of Blue Cedar lower diagnostic wing located Sonia Hale alive in a locked decommissioned sleep-study room. Recovered items include suppressed adverse-event binders, relabeled trial stock, and the victim's thumb drive.

options.label: Focus first on Sonia's personal circle
Lock down clinic access and camera records
Pull Sonia's devices and cloud drafts immediately
Interview night security despite the shaky first account
Review parking garage and hallway footage
Press staff on the freezer and infusion lot discrepancies
Process Records Annex B for trace evidence
Audit Sonia's export activity and trial workstation logs
Apply for a warrant on the lower diagnostic wing and Quill's office
